summary.intent: Fix a broken sessionDetails view in the Gossamer VS Code extension after a recent commit, then commit the fix and merge it into the gossamer-testing worktree.
summary.outcome: The root cause was identified (acquireVsCodeApi() moved to an inline HTML script that ran before VS Code injected it, silently breaking all postToExtension calls) and fixed across three files, but the commit/merge step was interrupted mid-execution.

repo: GossamerPanel uses static class fields (GossamerPanel.server, GossamerPanel.serverPort) so the server process survives panel close/reopen and is only killed on extension deactivate or process exit.
The mcp branch is the active development branch; testing is a separate worktree at ~/gossamer-testing used for integration/staging.
acquireVsCodeApi() in VS Code webviews must be called lazily from module-scope JS, not from an inline HTML script — the VS Code runtime may not have injected it yet when inline scripts run.

code.finding: acquireVsCodeApi() was incorrectly moved to window.__vscodeApi via an inline HTML script; reverted to lazy acquisition inside getApi() to ensure it is called after VS Code injects the API.
SessionDetailPanel was opened with ViewColumn.One; was a suspect but not the root cause of the 'nothing happens' symptom.
Minimize button moved from standalone inline HTML into React so it uses postToExtension through the properly acquired API.

workflow: When a VS Code webview action silently does nothing, the first thing to check is whether postToExtension is receiving a valid vscode API object — silent no-ops are a common failure mode.
Edits made in one worktree do not automatically appear in another; changes must be explicitly staged, committed, and then merged/applied to the target worktree.
TypeScript compile check (tsc --noEmit) is a fast way to validate changes before testing in extension host.

friction: User said 'cmp branch' but meant 'mcp branch', requiring a clarification round-trip before the merge could proceed.
Changes were accidentally staged and committed in the testing worktree instead of the mcp branch, requiring a diff-copy workflow to move them to the right branch.
No immediate error surface for the broken acquireVsCodeApi() call — it failed silently, making diagnosis require reading source rather than observing an error.
open_items: The commit of the three-file fix (vscodeApi.ts, GossamerPanel.ts, App.tsx) was not completed — the session was interrupted during the apply-diff-to-mcp step.
The merge of the fixed mcp branch into the testing worktree was not completed.
Consider whether ViewColumn.One in SessionDetailPanel.ts should be changed to ViewColumn.Beside to avoid layout conflicts.
